started_at_session: My Java program keeps crashing with NullPointerException. I created a Room object but calling describe() on it blows up. I've tried adding initializers but nothing works. Can you help me figure out what I'm missing?
student_message: My Java program keeps crashing with NullPointerException. I created a Room object but calling describe() on it blows up. I've tried adding initializers but nothing works. Can you help me figure out what I'm missing?

lp_diagnostic.wrong_model_description: The variable holds the object itself, not a pointer to it. There is no concept of indirection.
lp_diagnostic.wrong_model_origin: Variables contain values directly in mental model
lp_diagnostic.lp_rubric_current: Knows NPE happened, cannot say why. Cannot answer 'what does s hold after String s;'
lp_diagnostic.lp_rubric_target: Knows null means 'no object assigned'. Knows the fix is to initialise with new. Cannot trace the dereference mechanism.
expert_benchmark_key_ideas: Can trace: declaration creates a reference slot holding null.
new allocates a heap object and returns its address.
Method call dereferences the reference - follows the address to the heap object.
null dereference fails because there is no address to follow -> NPE.
Can predict which line throws NPE.

misconceptions.description: Student declares a reference variable but never instantiates the object
misconceptions.correction_strategy: Show reference vs object with memory diagram; always initialize with new

concept_info.label: null_pointer
concept_info.description: References, object creation. Error: NullPointerException when calling method on uninitialised reference.

wrong_models.wrong_belief: Declaring a variable creates the object. String s; creates a String object named s. The name and the thing are the same act.
wrong_models.origin: Python/scripting: name = thing is one act. Natural language: naming implies existence.

wrong_models.wrong_belief: null means empty or zero. Calling a method on null should return empty/default result, not crash.
wrong_models.origin: null is like 0 for numbers or empty string for strings - a valid default value

wrong_models.wrong_belief: The variable holds the object itself, not a pointer to it. There is no concept of indirection.
wrong_models.origin: Variables contain values directly in mental model

misconceptions.description: Student declares a reference variable but never instantiates the object

lp_rubric.L1: Knows NPE happened, cannot say why. Cannot answer 'what does s hold after String s;'
lp_rubric.L2: Knows null means 'no object assigned'. Knows the fix is to initialise with new. Cannot trace the dereference mechanism.
lp_rubric.L3: Can trace: declaration creates a reference slot holding null. new allocates a heap object and returns its address. Method call dereferences the reference - follows the address to the heap object. null dereference fails because there is no address to follow -> NPE. Can predict which line throws NPE.
lp_rubric.L4: Applies to all reference types. Understands why null checks are necessary before method calls. Can write null-safe code. Understands Optional as a design pattern.

theory_of_mind.reasoning: Student is engaged | likely to continue
